summary: Instructions (steps 13-16) for connecting the gun cable connector and wire feed control cable to the welder's wire feed mechanism, and for setting up DCEN (Direct Current Electrode Negative) polarity for flux-cored (gasless) welding, including correct cable socket connections and locking method.
text:
13. Loosen the Knob on the Wire Feed mechanism, then insert the Gun Cable Connector through the hole on the Welder front and into the socket on the Wire Feed.

14. Ensure that the Gun Cable Connector is fully inserted into the socket on the Wire Feed mechanism as shown, then tighten the Knob securely. If Connector is not fully inserted, the gas connection will leak, preventing shielding gas from reaching the welding arc.

NOTICE: To prevent damage, do not overtighten the Knob.

15. Insert the Wire Feed Control Cable through the hole on the Welder front and connect it to the Wire Feed Control Socket inside the machine, then tighten the lock ring on the Cable plug. Note that the plug on the Cable fits into the Socket in one specific orientation only.

16. DCEN Direct Current Electrode Negative Wire Setup for Flux-Cored (gasless) welding:
Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Positive (+) Socket. Plug Wire Feed Power Cable into Negative (–) Socket. Twist cables clockwise all the way to lock in place.

figures.caption: Incorrect – Connector not fully inserted
figures.description: Diagram of the Wire Feed Mechanism showing the Gun Cable Connector partially inserted into the socket, illustrating incorrect installation. Callouts label the Wire Feed Mechanism, Knob, and Gun Cable Connector.

key_facts: To connect the gun cable, loosen the Knob on the Wire Feed mechanism, insert the Gun Cable Connector through the hole on the welder front into the Wire Feed socket, then tighten the Knob securely.
If the Gun Cable Connector is not fully inserted, the gas connection will leak, preventing shielding gas from reaching the welding arc.
NOTICE: Do not overtighten the Knob on the Wire Feed mechanism, to prevent damage.
The Wire Feed Control Cable plug fits into its Socket in one specific orientation only; tighten the lock ring on the Cable plug after connecting.
DCEN (Direct Current Electrode Negative) setup is used for Flux-Cored (gasless) welding.
For DCEN setup: plug the Ground Clamp Cable into the Positive (+) socket, and plug the Wire Feed Power Cable into the Negative (–) socket.
Twist cables clockwise all the way to lock them in place in their sockets.
